preamble: The palatalization of the voiceless aspirated alveolar stop [tʰ] into the voiceless postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a common historical and allophonic process, particularly when followed by high front vowels or glides.

examples.note: Historical allophony where /t/ was aspirated and then palatalized before /i/.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
